context: Tests whether the agent correctly maps system requirement signals to appropriate patterns: CQRS for read/write asymmetry, monolith or synchronous services for strong consistency, event-driven for an event-heavy notification system, and service-per-compliance-boundary for PCI DSS isolation.

checklist.name: CQRS for read/write asymmetry
checklist.description: recommendation.md identifies CQRS as appropriate for the high read-to-write ratio, or explicitly maps read/write asymmetry to CQRS consideration

checklist.name: Strong consistency solution
checklist.description: recommendation.md recommends a monolith or synchronous service approach for the grade submission subsystem that requires strong consistency

checklist.name: Event-driven for notifications
checklist.description: recommendation.md identifies event-driven architecture as appropriate for the notification and course-event subsystem

checklist.name: Regulatory service isolation
checklist.description: recommendation.md recommends isolating the payment component as a separate service boundary to satisfy PCI DSS compliance requirements

checklist.name: Read/write asymmetry cited
checklist.description: recommendation.md explicitly cites the read-to-write ratio or read/write asymmetry as the signal driving the CQRS or read-model recommendation

checklist.name: Consistency requirement cited
checklist.description: recommendation.md explicitly cites the strong consistency requirement for grades as a reason to avoid eventual-consistency patterns for that subsystem

checklist.name: Compliance requirement cited
checklist.description: recommendation.md explicitly cites PCI DSS or regulatory isolation as the reason for the service boundary around payments

checklist.name: No CQRS for simple subsystems
checklist.description: recommendation.md does NOT recommend CQRS for the simpler, consistency-focused subsystems (e.g. grade submission) that do not have read/write asymmetry

checklist.name: Requirement signals are the basis
checklist.description: recommendation.md anchors each pattern choice to a specific requirement signal rather than general preference
